div.head: The Ambassador in Japan (Grew) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Sir: With reference to the Embassy’s telegram No. 1213 of August 11, 8 p.m.,Not printed. containing a translation of Foreign Office Note No. 87/–American I, concerning the unauthorized flight of Japanese naval aircraft over the island of Guam, I have the honor to enclose, for the record, a translation of the note in question,Not printed. complete in every detail.

It will be noted that the Foreign Office considers that sufficient amends have been made for the case of May 27, 1941; that an error
on the part of the flight officer on June 12 [11] (not June 11 [12] as was mentioned by the DepartmentSee telegram No. 344, June 20, 8 p.m., to the Ambassador in Japan, p. 182.) is acknowledged; and that with reference to the incidents of June 14 and June 19, it is alleged that the “territorial air” was not violated, although an admission is made that flights so near to Guam are improper.
